title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: This article discusses how a study by Fabio Giglietto, a professor at the University of Urbino, titled "A Pretty Blunt Approach," was referenced in a Report TV episode and an European Parliament inquiry. The study highlights a 72% reduction in visibility for Italian parliamentarians on Facebook, contrasting with increased exposure for extremist content. Fabio Giglietto is quoted directly regarding these findings.
description: This article from vivereurbino.it reports on a study by Fabio Giglietto, a professor at the University of Urbino, concerning the impact of algorithms on democracy and social media visibility for Italian parliamentarians and extremist content, as referenced in recent public inquiries.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni - Lumsanews
snippet: This article from Lumsanews reports on a study authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto that analyzed over 2.5 million Facebook posts from 2021 to 2025. The study concludes that Meta's algorithmic suppression is less effective against the very actors it intended to target, specifically extremist and conspiratorial content, and even promotes them.
description: This article from Lumsanews reports on a study authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto, which analyzed Facebook posts to conclude that Meta's algorithmic suppression is ineffective against extremist and conspiratorial content, potentially even promoting it.

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: This news article reports on a study published by the University of Urbino, co-authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto, which found that Meta's algorithms on Facebook penalize official political content while boosting extremist and anti-European content. The article quotes findings from his research.
description: News article reporting on a University of Urbino study co-authored by Fabio Giglietto, which found Facebook algorithms boost extremist content over official politics, quoting his research findings.
